title: Local disk usage spikes when using skopeo copy to transfer a large image tarball to a remote registry
body: I've encountered an issue where my local disk usage increases dramatically when I use skopeo copy to transfer a large image tarball, around 50GB in size, to a remote registry. The local disk usage goes up by approximately 50GB during the process. Could you provide insight into why this happens and if there's a way to mitigate such a large increase in disk usage?

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

Assuming “a large image tarball” refers to `docker-archive:` sources (please be specific!), consuming a `docker-archive:` tarball requires accessing the individual components of that archive in ~random order. So, if the archive is compressed, we decompress it once to disk, so that the individual components can be read quickly. The alternative would be to decompress the whole archive for every file that needs to be read from the archive.

In general, `docker-archive:` is a compatibility/interoperability mechanism, and it’s inherently and unfixably inefficient; this is one of the examples.

For any workloads where performance is a concern, I strongly recommend using a registry, not the docker-archive format.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Please reopen if there is anything unexpected happening.
